explanation_en: 'Grundlage' means 'basis' or 'foundation'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The basis of the contract is solid.

explanation_en: 'Grundlage' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ für eine gute Zusammenarbeit ist Vertrauen.
question.en: The foundation for good collaboration is trust.

explanation_en: 'Grundlage' means 'foundation'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: We can build on this foundation.

explanation_en: 'auf dieser Grundlage' — 'auf' with dative for static location. Feminine dative: 'dieser Grundlage'.
'Haltung' means 'attitude' or 'posture'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The government's attitude is unclear.

explanation_en: 'Haltung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Seine _____ zu diesem Problem ist entschieden.
question.en: His attitude to this problem is decided.

explanation_en: 'Haltung' means 'attitude'. After 'seine' (feminine accusative used as possessive), the noun is unchanged.

question.de: Ich bewundere ihre klare _____ zu diesem Thema.
question.en: I admire her clear attitude on this topic.

explanation_en: 'bewundern' takes the accusative. 'ihre klare Haltung' — feminine accusative is same as nominative.
'Leistung' means 'performance' or 'achievement'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The performance of the team was exceptional.

explanation_en: 'Leistung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: Die _____ der Schüler hat sich verbessert.
question.en: The pupils' performance has improved.

explanation_en: 'Leistung' means 'performance'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: We need to increase the performance.

explanation_en: 'steigern' takes the accusative. Feminine: 'die Leistung' (unchanged in accusative).
'Maßnahme' means 'measure' or 'step' taken to address a situation. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The measure is urgently necessary.

explanation_en: 'Maßnahme' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The measure was taken immediately.

explanation_en: 'Maßnahme' means 'measure'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: The government is taking strict measures.

explanation_en: 'ergreifen' takes accusative. Plural: 'strenge Maßnahmen' (plural of Maßnahme adds -n).
'Mehrheit' means 'majority'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The majority voted for the proposal.

explanation_en: 'Mehrheit' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The majority of the population is in favour.

explanation_en: 'Mehrheit' means 'majority'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.de: Das Gesetz wurde mit knapper _____ verabschiedet.
question.en: The law was passed with a slim majority.

explanation_en: 'mit knapper Mehrheit' — 'mit' takes dative. Feminine dative: 'knapper Mehrheit' (adj. takes strong ending without article).
'Schicht' has three meanings: a layer (of something), a social class, or a work shift. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.de: _____ Schicht beginnt um sechs Uhr morgens.
question.en: The shift begins at six in the morning.

explanation_en: 'Schicht' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: He works the night shift.

explanation_en: 'Schicht' means 'shift' (work). 'Nachtschicht' is a compound: night + shift.

question.en: Social class determines many life opportunities.

explanation_en: 'bestimmt' takes accusative. 'Die soziale Schicht' as subject, but 'Lebenschancen' as object. 'Schicht' here is nominative subject.
'Tendenz' means 'tendency' or 'trend'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The trend is clearly recognisable.

explanation_en: 'Tendenz' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The trend towards more online work is growing.

explanation_en: 'Tendenz' means 'trend'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: We are observing a worrying trend.

explanation_en: 'beobachten' takes the accusative. 'eine beunruhigende Tendenz' — feminine indefinite accusative: 'eine'.
'Verfassung' means 'constitution' (legal document) or 'condition' (physical/mental state). Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.en: The constitution protects fundamental rights.

explanation_en: 'Verfassung' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.en: The constitution of Germany was passed in 1949.

explanation_en: 'Verfassung' means 'constitution'. It is the feminine subject here.

question.en: He is in good physical condition.

explanation_en: 'in guter Verfassung' — 'in' with dative. Feminine dative: 'guter Verfassung' (adj. takes strong ending without article).
'Wirklichkeit' means 'reality'. Feminine, takes 'die'.

question.de: _____ Wirklichkeit ist oft komplizierter als die Theorie.
question.en: Reality is often more complicated than theory.

explanation_en: 'Wirklichkeit' is feminine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'die'.

question.de: In der _____ sieht das anders aus.
question.en: In reality it looks different.

explanation_en: 'Wirklichkeit' means 'reality'. After 'in der' (dative feminine), the noun is unchanged.

question.de: Er verdrängt die _____ und lebt in einer Traumwelt.
question.en: He suppresses reality and lives in a dream world.

explanation_en: 'verdrängen' takes accusative. Feminine: 'die Wirklichkeit' (unchanged in accusative).
'Ausmaß' means 'extent' or 'scale'. Neuter, takes 'das'.

question.de: _____ Ausmaß des Problems ist noch nicht klar.
question.en: The extent of the problem is not yet clear.

explanation_en: 'Ausmaß' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.en: The scale of the catastrophe was alarming.

explanation_en: 'Ausmaß' means 'scale'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.en: We underestimate the extent of the changes.

explanation_en: 'unterschätzen' takes accusative. Neuter: 'das Ausmaß' (unchanged in accusative).
'Bewusstsein' means 'consciousness' (being awake) or 'awareness' (knowing something). Neuter, takes 'das'.

question.en: Awareness of the problem is growing.

explanation_en: 'Bewusstsein' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ für Umweltschutz muss gestärkt werden.
question.en: Awareness of environmental protection must be strengthened.

explanation_en: 'Bewusstsein' means 'awareness'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.de: Er verlor das _____ nach dem Unfall.
question.en: He lost consciousness after the accident.

explanation_en: 'verlieren' takes accusative. Neuter: 'das Bewusstsein' (unchanged in accusative).
'Gleichgewicht' means 'balance' or 'equilibrium'. Neuter, takes 'das'.

question.de: _____ Gleichgewicht zwischen Angebot und Nachfrage ist wichtig.
question.en: The balance between supply and demand is important.

explanation_en: 'Gleichgewicht' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ zwischen Arbeit und Freizeit ist schwer zu halten.
question.en: The balance between work and leisure is hard to maintain.

explanation_en: 'Gleichgewicht' means 'balance'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.en: He lost his balance and fell.

explanation_en: 'verlieren' takes accusative. Neuter: 'das Gleichgewicht' (unchanged in accusative).
'Konzept' means 'concept' or 'plan'. Neuter, takes 'das'.

question.en: The concept is very well thought out.

explanation_en: 'Konzept' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ des Projekts muss noch verfeinert werden.
question.en: The concept of the project still needs to be refined.

explanation_en: 'Konzept' means 'concept'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.de: Wir entwickeln ein neues _____ für die Kampagne.
question.en: We are developing a new concept for the campaign.

explanation_en: After 'ein neues' (neuter accusative adjective), 'Konzept' is unchanged.
